embed_dtype: float32 (bge-small; NOT the spec dim-768 float8 I/O basis)
candidate_generation: cosine kNN via cuVS CAGRA (bounded pool, TR-1 respected)

topological_scorers.node2vec_skip_reason: training node2vec on 6.9M nodes / 224M edges = multi-billion-token walk corpus + skip-gram training, multi-hour job, out of scope for the cheapest-test remit; CN/AA are training-free

metric_kind: link RECOVERY / precision@k on the cosine pool (reconstruction proxy), NOT held-out link prediction

overlap_at_k.note: fraction of each source's top-k reranked candidates that are already out-edges (link recovery / precision@k, a reconstruction proxy — NOT held-out prediction); candidate pool is the cosine top-`pool` for ALL columns, so cosine_only reproduces the 0.1101 lower bound and the rest are pure reranks of the identical pool. common_neighbor_only / adamic_adar_only are the LEAKAGE-CORRECTED numbers (leave-out-the-positives); leaky_* are the uncorrected versions kept for comparison. popularity_indeg_only is a degree-prior control. pool_base_rate is the linked fraction over the whole pool (ceiling on recovery).

leakage_control.protocol: leave-out-the-positives: A's out-edges that are also candidates in the pool (== the test positives) are removed from succ(A) before computing CN/AA, so scoring A->B cannot borrow A's OTHER pooled true out-edges as shared neighbours
leakage_control.note: corrected is the honest number; (leaky - corrected) is the reconstruction inflation from interlinked pooled positives

popularity_control.reranker: order the pool by candidate global in-degree indeg(B)
popularity_control.note: if this matches/beats Adamic-Adar the topological claim collapses to a degree prior; if AA clears it, structure adds signal beyond popularity

significance.test: paired bootstrap (4000 resamples) + Wilcoxon signed-rank over the 5000 per-source (AA_corrected - cosine) overlap@10 deltas

orthogonality.spearman_note: rank correlation of the cosine vs Adamic-Adar orderings within each pool; high => the two rank the SAME topical-proximity construct, i.e. AA is a sharper ranker of the same signal, not an orthogonal modality
orthogonality.recovery_note: per-source count of true out-edges each ranker surfaces in its top-k that the other misses; a nonzero AA-unique count means topology recovers some links the embedding buries

verdict: MODEST SIGNAL: leakage-corrected Adamic-Adar 0.1246 beats cosine 0.11 (Δ+0.0146, 13.3% relative; paired 95% CI [+0.0118, +0.0176] excludes 0, Wilcoxon p=2.64e-24) and clears a popularity-only reranker (0.0955).
gating: compute-bound-ish (dim-384 f32) link-recovery / reranking test (reconstruction proxy), NOT a latency claim (ADR-0017: I/O regime is expected-fail) and NOT held-out prediction. Real 224M-edge graph, bounded reranking pool.
